The scale drawing shows the positions of two villages, $P$ and $Q$.

The scale is 1\,cm represents 0.5\,km.

(a) 
t Find the actual distance between village $P$ and village $Q$.

t Measure the bearing of village $Q$ from village $P$.

Find the value of $x$ and the value of $y$.

Samira picks one of these cards at random and replaces it.

(a) 
t Find the probability that she picks an odd number.

t Samira repeats this 35 times.

Calculate the number of times Samira is expected to pick an odd number.

(a) 
t Translate triangle $T$ by the vector $\displaystyle\binom{0}{-2}$.

t Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $U$.

\[
11 \qquad 8 \qquad 5 \qquad 2
\]

(a) 
t Find the next term of this sequence.

t Find the $n$th term of this sequence.
Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 36 and 54.

(a) 
t $\overrightarrow{AC} = 2\overrightarrow{AB}$

Find the coordinates of the point $C$.

Find the value of $k$.

t $P$ is a point on $AB$.

$AP : PB = 1 : 3$

Find the position vector of $P$.

The length of the arc is $n\pi$\,cm.

Find the value of $n$.

(a) 
t Write $0.007\,08$ in standard form.

t Work out $(3.8 \times 10^{22}) + (3.8 \times 10^{23})$.

Give your answer in standard form.

$QR$ is a diameter.

Find angle $PRQ$.

Give geometrical reasons for your answer.

Angle $PRQ = $ ...\ because ...

...

(a) 
t 100 students solve a puzzle.

The table shows information about the time taken by each student to solve the puzzle.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ seconds) & $20 < t \leqslant 40$ & $40 < t \leqslant 60$ & $60 < t \leqslant 100$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 30 & 40 & 30 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Work out an estimate of the mean.

 Complete the histogram to show the information in the table.

t 80 adults solve the same puzzle as the students.

The cumulative frequency table shows information about the time taken by each adult to solve the puzzle.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ seconds) & $t \leqslant 20$ & $t \leqslant 40$ & $t \leqslant 60$ & $t \leqslant 80$ & $t \leqslant 100$ & $t \leqslant 120$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 0 & 12 & 36 & 60 & 74 & 80 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram.

 Use your cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate for

\begin{parts}

(b) the median

(c) the lower quartile.

\end{parts}

tex: Write $0.2\dot{5}$ as a fraction.

The diagram shows the graph of $y = \dfrac{2}{x} - 1$.

(a) 
t Write down the coordinates of the point where the graph crosses the $x$-axis.

t Write down the equation of each asymptote.

t By drawing a suitable straight line on the grid, solve $\dfrac{2}{x} - x - 1 = 0$.

The diagram shows a solid made by joining a hemisphere to a cylinder.

The radius of both the hemisphere and the cylinder is 6\,cm.

The height of the cylinder is 5\,cm.

Find the **total** surface area of the solid.

Give your answer in terms of $\pi$.

Find the value of

(a) 
t $125^{\frac{2}{3}}$

t $4^{-\frac{5}{2}}$

(a) 
t $\dfrac{9}{\sqrt{3}}$

Rationalise the denominator.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

t $(5 - \sqrt{2})(1 + 3\sqrt{2}) = c + k\sqrt{2}$

Find the value of $c$ and the value of $k$.

Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.

(a) 
t $\dfrac{5a}{6} \times \dfrac{3b}{a}$

t $\dfrac{p}{2} + \dfrac{3t}{4}$

t $\dfrac{2}{x-2} - \dfrac{3}{x+1}$

$y \propto \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{x}}$

(a) 
t When $x = 9$, $y = 2$.

Find the value of $y$ when $x = 36$.

t When $x$ is increased by a factor of 4, the value of $y$ changes by a factor of $p$.

Find the value of $p$.

The diagram shows the graph of $y = 3x - x^3$.

The graph crosses the $x$-axis at $A$, at $O$ and at $B$.

The turning points of the graph are at $P$ and at $Q$.

(a) 
t Find the $x$-coordinate of $A$ and the $x$-coordinate of $B$.

Give your answers as exact values.

t
(i)  Differentiate $3x - x^3$.

 Find the coordinates of $P$ and $Q$.

(a) 
t Write down the exact value of $\tan 60°$.

t Solve $2\sin x - 1 = 0$ for $0° \leqslant x \leqslant 360°$.

In the diagram, $OA$ is parallel to $BC$.

$BC = 3OA$

$M$ is the midpoint of $AC$.

The position vector of $A$ is $\mathbf{a}$ and the position vector of $B$ is $\mathbf{b}$.

Find the position vector of $M$.

Give your answer in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbf{b}$, in its simplest form.

The line $y = 7x + 3$ intersects the curve $y = x^2 + 5x - 12$ at the points $A$ and $B$.

Find the coordinates of $A$ and $B$.
